msgs.text:
I treated the Turn 9–16 “dynamic tame-inertia” word as the only still-viable candidate; the earlier words are not just unproved but explicitly refuted in the prior file. The prompt’s required syntax is the four-generator tame format with distinguished sigma,tau, implicit tau^sigma=tau^2, and exponents reduced modulo M=85667662080.The prior investigations isolate the current candidate and leave, in Turn 16, one residual commutator-valued Frobenius-action bit in the unramified quadratic case.

The candidate remains:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently,r=xσ−πxσπx−1(xτ−2π)π ((yτ)π)σy−1,withπ=π2 ≡40491355905,−π≡45176306175,−2π ≡50932630783(modM).The external literature is consistent with the prior-turn framing: Jannsen–Wingberg’s known method for p=2 works by checking Demuškin groups over finite normal tame subextensions together with the finite tame action; Neukirch’s report explicitly describes that Demuškin-formation uniqueness step.Numdam Jarden–Shusterman record that Diekert’s p=2 analogue does not cover K=Q2, and that the minimal generator and relation counts for Gal(K) are [K:Qp]+2 and [K:Qp]+1, respectively.TAU Mathematics My new calculation addresses Turn 16’s remaining unramified-quadratic commutator bit.

Let L/Q2 be the unramified quadratic extension, L=Q2 (ω) withω2+ω+1=0,and let F denote arithmetic Frobenius, so F(ω)=ω2=−1−ω. In the Turn 16 notation for the unramified quadratic kernel U, takea0=x0,a1=x1,b=y0x1,c=S=σ2.

The candidate gives, modulo U3,q=a0[2]+a1[2]+[b,c],and Turn 16’s only remaining ambiguity was whether the Frobenius lift satisfies

F(b)=borF(b)=b[a0,a1]after the already-matched Uab/4 action. The candidate’s Magnus calculation said the second, nonzero, case occurs. I now get the same answer from explicit local Kummer arithmetic.

Use the square-class representatives m=−1,u=1+2ω,v=1+2(1+ω)=3+2ω.Then

F(v)=1−2ω,vF(v)=7≡−1in L×/L×2,so F(v)≡mv in square classes. A direct Hilbert-symbol computation in the basis (2,m,u,v), with entries in F2 and 1 meaning the nontrivial Hilbert symbol, gives0010001111100101.

Under the Turn 16 class-field identification c↔2,a0+a1 ↔m,y0 ↔u,a0 ↔v,one getsa1 ↔F(v),b=y0+a1 ↔umv.Dualizing the above Hilbert matrix gives the Kummer-dual classesa0∗=v,a1∗=F(v),b∗=2,c∗=muv.

Now form the D4 -extension attached to the pair (a0∗,a1∗)=(v,F(v)). Since (v,F(v))L=1, the usual Kummer D4 -construction applies. Putα=v,β=F(v).

The key norm identity is extremely simple:F(v)=4−v=(−2−α)(−2+α).

Thus, setting θ=−2−α,the D4 -extension is generated over L by α, β, δ=θ.

The central involution in this D4 -extension is dual to the commutator [a0,a1].Apply Frobenius. Since F(α)=β on the biquadratic layer,F(θ)=−2−β.

Inside B=L(α,β),θF(θ)=(−2−α)(−2−β)=4+2α+2β+αβ=2(1+2α+β)2.

ThereforeθF(θ)≡2in B×/B×2.

The class 2 is not in the span of v and F(v), so2∈/B. Thus Frobenius does not carry δ to an element of the same D4 -field without adjoining the square root of 2; explicitly one can take

F(δ)=δ2(1+2α+β).This is exactly the missing commutator bit. The square root2 is the Kummer character b∗, while the central D4 -involution is [a0,a1]. Hence the arithmetic Frobenius transport of the D4 -generator is twisted by b∗, which dually says

F(b)=b[a0,a1](modU3).So Turn 16’s candidate-side nonzero residual bit matches the arithmetic D4 -descent calculation.This does not yet make the whole presentation a theorem, but it closes the specific one-bit gap left by Turn 16 in the first genuinely 2-primary Frobenius-action test. The remaining issue is now the global formation step: package the finite-tame Demuškin, Kummer-module, cup-product, Bockstein, and U/U3 -action checks into a dyadic analogue of the Jannsen–Wingberg uniqueness argument.

ESTABLISHED: The Turn 9–16 candidate survives a new arithmetic test. In the unramified quadratic case, the last residual Frobenius-action bit is nonzero: the explicit D4 -extension attached to (v,F(v)) requires a2 -twist under Frobenius, which is dual to F(b)=b[a0,a1](modU3). This matches the candidate’s Magnus-side computation.

GAP: A complete proof still needs a formal dyadic formation/rigidity theorem showing that the compatible finite-tame data, now including this U/U3

Frobenius bit, force the full profinite group to be GQ2. I have not proved that global uniqueness statement here.

NEXT: Extend this D4 -descent computation from the unramified quadratic test to arbitrary 2-primary unramified degree, then formulate the resulting condition as an explicit axiom in a dyadic Koch/Demuškin formation.[BREAKTHROUGH]
